review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to denial-of-service protection. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor adjustment was made for clarity and specificity in the pod security context function.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names specify the exact capability being restricted, such as NET_RAW, for clarity.
title: Denial-Of-Service Protection - Denial - of - service protection | restrict ability to attack other systems rest
step1_reasoning: The control SC-5(1) focuses on restricting the ability to launch denial-of-service attacks on other systems. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed by implementing NetworkPolicies to restrict egress traffic, ensuring that Pods cannot communicate freely with external systems. Additionally, configuring Pod security contexts to restrict network capabilities can prevent Pods from using network features that could be exploited for attacks. Admission controllers can be used to deny Pods that attempt to use privileged ports or capabilities that could facilitate such attacks.
